JPMorgan says Ethereum likely to keep facing ‘intense competition’ from other networks
data.description:
Ethereum is likely to continue facing "intense competition" from rival blockchains, according to JPMorgan analysts.

While the broader crypto market surged around the U.S. election, Ethereum's native cryptocurrency, ether, has lagged behind — not only underperforming bitcoin but also other altcoins, JPMorgan analysts led by managing director Nikolaos Panigirtzoglou wrote in a report on Wednesday. Ether's share of the total crypto market cap has now dropped to a four-year low, highlighting growing pressure from competitors, the analysts said.

Why is Ethereum underperforming?

The analysts point to two main reasons: rising competition from blockchains such as Solana and Layer 2 networks offering lower fees and greater scalability, and Ethereum's lack of a strong narrative compared to Bitcoin's positioning as a store of value.

Even after Ethereum's Dencun upgrade introduced blobs to reduce fees and improve scalability, activity has increasingly migrated from Ethereum's main network to Layer 2 networks, weakening the base chain, the analysts said.

Rising competition has also driven top decentralized applications to migrate to their own application-specific chains for better performance and lower costs. Uniswap, dYdX, and Hyperliquid have all made this move, with Uniswap's upcoming shift to Unichain being particularly significant, according to the analysts. As one of Ethereum's largest gas consumers, Uniswap's departure could reduce Ethereum's fee revenue and increase the risk of the network becoming inflationary, as fewer transactions would mean lower token burn, the analysts said.

Despite these challenges, Ethereum still leads in stablecoins, DeFi and tokenization areas, though its ability to maintain this dominance remains to be seen, according to the analysts.

To boost institutional adoption, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin and the Ethereum Foundation recently reportedly invested in Etherealize, a startup founded by former Wall Street trader Vivek Raman. Etherealize focuses on promoting Ethereum to financial firms, highlighting use cases such as tokenization while also developing products to simplify Ethereum's integration with banks.

While tokenization could drive Ethereum's institutional demand, "competition from other networks is likely to remain intense in the foreseeable future," the analysts concluded.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. data.title: Al Ain Alahlia Insurance to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Al Ain Alahlia Insurance (ADX:ALAIN) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 11:00 am local time on Feb. 13 due to its board meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-based insurance and reinsurance services provider will resume trading after it discloses the meeting results, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Cocoa Futures at 3-Week Low
data.description:
Cocoa futures traded around $10,500 per tonne, holding close to their lowest since mid-January, as concerns over demand resurfaced.

Mondelez International, a key player in the chocolate industry, warned of a potential global slowdown in chocolate demand, attributing a larger-than-expected drop in annual profit to "unprecedented" cocoa costs.

At the same time, beneficial rainfall in the key producing region of West Africa has boosted cocoa tree growth and is putting downward pressure on cocoa prices.

Farmers in the Ivory Coast and Ghana report that the recent rains have improved crop conditions and prompted the re-flowering of cocoa trees.

data.title: Japan's Itochu 9-mth net up 11%, firm weighs investing in Seven & i
data.description:
Japanese trading house Itochu posted on Thursday an increase of 11% in nine-month net profit to December of 676.5 billion yen ($4.44 billion) from the corresponding period a year earlier.

The company kept unchanged its full-year profit forecast of 880 billion yen for the fiscal year ending in March.

Itochu is considering whether to invest in Seven & i Holdings , but no decision has yet been made, Chief Financial Officer Tsuyoshi Hachimura told a press conference.

"We have built a track record of firmly implementing investment criteria and only investing in projects that contribute to future growth," Hachimura said.

There was nothing special about the investment in Seven & i, he added, saying Itochu would only invest if stakeholders were satisfied.

Seven & i, the operator of the 7-Eleven convenience store chain, is trying to fend off a $47-billion buyout offer from Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (ACT).

After ACT's bid, the Seven & i group's founding family started talks to take the company private for an estimated $58 billion, in what would be the largest management buyout in Japanese history.

Some media have said the family sought cooperation from Itochu, which was considering an investment of 1 trillion yen in the company.

Hachimura suggested there are no funding issues, saying it has improved shareholders' equity over the past years and its credit rating has gone up.

($1=152.2100 yen)

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Qualcomm, Arm, Skyworks, Ford, Strategy, Amazon, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures traded higher Thursday as investors looked past worries over tariffs and turned their attention to earnings, particularly from the tech sector.

These stocks were poised to make moves Thursday:

Qualcomm posted fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.75 a share, beating Wall Street estimates of $2.46, as revenue jumped 17% to $11.67 billion. Revenue at the company's QCT semiconductor business rose 20% in the period to $10.1 billion. But intellectual property licensing revenue of $1.54 billion missed estimates of $1.56 billion. Qualcomm said it expects fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.38 to $2.58 a share on revenue of $10.3 billion to $11.2 billion. Shares of the chip maker were falling 4.8% in premarket trading.

Chip designer Arm Holdings reported fiscal third-quarter adjusted earnings that beat analysts' estimates but its guidance for its fourth quarter matched forecasts, disappointing investors and sending the stock down 4%. For the fourth quarter, Arm said it expects adjusted earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.23 billion at the midpoint of its guidance. Wall Street anticipates fourth-quarter earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.22 billion.

Skyworks Solutions plunged 29% after the wireless chip maker said it expected its share of the business to supply Apple, its biggest customer, with radio frequency components would decline by 20% to 25%. The drop, Skyworks said in a conference call, would begin impacting revenue in its fiscal fourth quarter and throughout fiscal 2026. The company also named Philip Brace, who most recently served as CEO of 5G mobile company Inseego, as its next president and CEO.

Ford Motor fell 4.8% after the auto maker posted fourth-quarter operating profit of $2.1 billion on sales of $48.2 billion, better than analysts' estimates on both metrics, but its forecast for operating profit this year of between $7 billion and $8.5 billion missed expectations of about $9.3 billion. Ford also said it expects breakeven adjusted earnings before interest and taxes in the first quarter because of "lower wholesales and unfavorable mix," and expenses related to the launch of some major U.S. assembly plants.

MicroStrategy, the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, rose 1.3% in premarket trading. The company, which announced Wednesday it now was doing business as Strategy, posted a fourth-quarter loss of $670.8 million, or $3.03 a share. Revenue fell 3% to $120.7 million and missed analysts' estimates of $122.4 million. The company also said it saw its largest-ever increase in quarterly Bitcoin holdings in the period, "culminating in the acquisition of 218,887 bitcoins acquired for $20.5 billion" since the third quarter. The company also said it has so far bought $20 billion of Bitcoin under a three-year plan of buying $42 billion in the cryptocurrency.

Molina Healthcare fell 8.4% after fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.05 a share missed analysts' expectations of $5.88 and the healthcare-services provider said it expected 2025 adjusted earnings of at least $24.50 a share, below analysts' estimates of $25.82.

Meta Platforms rose for a 13th-straight session on Wednesday, the stock's longest winning streak on record. Shares of the parent of Facebook and Instagram also set an intraday high of $718.14, and closed at a record high of $704.87. In premarket trading Thursday, Meta was up 0.4% at $707.85.

Earnings reports are expected Thursday from Amazon.com, Eli Lilly, Honeywell International, ConocoPhillips, Philip Morris International, Neurocrine Biosciences, Bristol Myers Squibb, Fortinet, Air Products, Cloudflare, Roblox, Hershey, and Take-Two Interactive Software.

Amazon.com was up 0.7% in premarket trading. Analysts expect the tech and online retailing giant to report fourth-quarter earnings of $1.49 a share on revenue of $187.3 billion. Wall Street will be watching Amazon's web services business closely after rival tech companies' cloud segments posted disappointing results. Amazon's report is scheduled for after the closing bell Thursday.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Orsted's Lower Spending Plans Allay Balance Sheet Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Orsted's cut to capital expenditure combined with its commitment to sell 70 billion-80 billion Danish kroner in assets is likely to bring reassurance on the balance sheet and credit metrics, UBS's Mark Freshney writes. However, a 10% rise in costs on a like-for-like basis is a negative for the Danish renewable-energy company, he adds. UBS says it would appear that the spending cuts relate to delaying or avoiding new projects between 2026 and 2030, so the EBITDA impact is long term. The full-year 2025 EBITDA guidance is very close to the bank's forecasts and only 3% below its forecasts for 2026. "Scaling back the business given cost pressures and no equity raise is a positive surprise." Shares trade 5.9% higher at 297.70 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex fell 0.3% to 78,058.16, while the broader NSE index declined 0.4% to 23,603.35, dragged down by rate-sensitive financial and auto stocks as caution prevailed a day ahead of the Reserve Bank of India's rate decision.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 87.5775, as a dip in Asian peers added to the prevailing bearish outlook on the currency a day before a crucial monetary policy decision.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN071034G=CC) was quoted at 100.9125 rupees, with the yield was largely unchanged at 6.6577%, ahead of the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy decision.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 1 basis point at 6.315%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was flat at 6.06%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.85% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.30%.

data.title: Update: NCR Atleos Expands ATM Network Into Italy
data.description:
(Updates with the latest stock moves in the last paragraph.)

NCR Atleos said Thursday it has expanded its automatic teller machine network into Italy.

The ATMs will operate under the Cashzone brand in Italy, the 13th country in which NCR Atleos has a presence, the company said.

Shares of the company were up more than 5% in Thursday's premarket activity.

data.title: Australia's Goods Balance Drops in December 2024
data.description:
Australia's trade surplus dropped sharply in December 2024 as imports significantly outweighed exports.

The balance on goods fell to AU$5.09 billion from AU$6.79 in November 2024, according to data published by the Australian Bureau of Statistics on Thursday.

Exports climbed 1.1% from the prior month on the back of the growth of rural goods exports, as well as metal ores and coal. On the other hand, imports soared 5.9% month on month, the highest in fifteen months, as capital goods imports jumped on a monthly basis, the ABS said.

Westpac's senior economist, Mantas Vanagas, said that although Australian exports doubled year on year in the US and grew in China and other countries in Europe during the month, there has been a noticeable drop in exports to New Zealand and other Asian countries.

As for the moment, there is uncertainty on how Australia's trade flow will be affected by escalating trade tensions after US President Donald Trump announced new tariffs against China, Mexico and Canada. There is also the possibility of these tariffs further hiking in China, with the 10% levies being described as an "opening salvo".

"While Australia is unlikely to be among the countries at the top of the President Trump's list for import tariffs, sharp changes in the trade flows in recent months suggest that Australia will not be completely immune to global forces driving international trade," Vanagas said

data.title: Swiss biotech BioVersys gets $235 million valuation after completing flotation
data.description:
Swiss biotech BioVersys, which develops treatments for serious infections caused by drug resistant bacteria, has completed its initial public offering with an implied market valuation of 213 million Swiss francs ($235.46 million).

Just over 2.2 million shares were placed by the Basel-based company in the all-primary offering at 36 francs per share, it said on Thursday.

Trading is due to start on Friday, with the stock expected to be included on the Swiss Performance Index on February 10.

BioVersys researches and develops novel antibacterial products for serious life-threatening infections caused by multidrug-resistant (MDR) bacteria.

It raised 90 million francs from the IPO, which will used towards the development of its BV100 treatment for infections linked to ventilator-associated pneumonia and bloodstream infections.

Some of the money will be used for its tuberculosis product alpibectir, which BioVersys is working on with GSK .

CEO Marc Gitzinger said the IPO was the next stage in the company's growth strategy.

"This support enables us to move full speed ahead with BV100 into the Phase 3 program, advance alpibectir into additional Phase 2 trials in collaboration with GSK and further progress our early stage pipeline," he said.

($1 = 0.9046 Swiss francs)

data.title: Jiangsu New Energy Development Controlling Shareholder-Led Consortium Wins Four Power Projects in Jiangsu Province
data.description:
Jiangsu New Energy Development said a consortium led by its controlling shareholder secured offshore wind power project rights in China's Jiangsu province.

The Guoxin Group-led consortium won four projects with a total capacity of 1.55 million kilowatts, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the new energy company closed 6% higher Thursday.

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Find Support from Saudi Aramco's Price Hike, but Trump Policies Still Weigh on Prices
data.description:
Crude oil prices saw gains after state-controlled Saudi Aramco sharply raised oil prices for March.

Brent crude rose 0.5% to US$75.01/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude gained 0.7% to US$71.54/b at last look early Thursday. This comes after prices slipped as a large increase in U.S. crude and gasoline stockpiles signaled demand weakness, Reuters said in a Thursday report.

Aramco cited rising demand from China and India and disruptions to Russian supply amid Western sanctions.

"We can expect significant volatility in pricing over the coming weeks and months as markets scramble to weigh the impact of Trump's new policy positions, not least regarding tariff measures," Reuters quoted analysts from BMI as saying.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea's Top Banks to Tighten Household Loan Growth in 2025
data.description:
KB Financial Group's KB Kookmin, Shinhan Financial Group's Shinhan Bank, Hana Financial Group's Hana Bank, Woori Financial Group's Woori Bank, and NH Nonghyup plan to limit household loan growth to 14 trillion won this year, down from 14.68 trillion won in 2024, Yonhap News reported Wednesday, citing data submitted to Rep. Lee In-young.

The plan by South Korea's top five banks, which excludes policy loans for the underprivileged, follows tighter regulations and declining borrowing amid global uncertainties, with household loans dropping for the first time in nine months, the report said.

Financial authorities continue to urge lenders to adopt stricter lending measures to curb household debt and stabilize home prices, the report said.

Shares of KB Financial Group plunged nearly 7%, and those of Hana Financial Group and Woori Financial Group fell nearly 1% each, at market close on Thursday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Lotus Bakeries drops on production capacity constraints
data.description:
** Shares of Belgian cookie maker Lotus Bakeries NV fall 5.7%

** Share on track for its worst day since Jan. 9, 2024 when it closed 6% lower

** Co reported FY results broadly in line with expectations, but limitations in production capacity hindered future growth

** Analysts at Degroof Petercam note that while Lotus' FY results were largely in line with expectations, the company's volume growth for Biscoff is limited to 10% due to capacity constraints

** "Results were strong but the limited production could hinder top-line growth in the short term," the Degroof Petercam analysts comment, maintaining their "Hold" rating for the stock

** "It is clear that following years of firm growth, Lotus literally faces struggles to produce enough Biscoff, limited 2025 growth to 10% in terms of volumes," they add, suggesting that the company's stock didn't fully meet high expectations

** Up to previous session's close, shares down 5.6% YTD

data.title: Slovenia's CPI rose to 2.0% y/y in January
data.description:
Slovenia's annual consumer price inflation came in at 2.0% in January, according to data released by the Statistical Office (SURS) on Thursday, up from 1.9% in December.

Group transport prices, which rose by 3.0%, and food and non-alcoholic beverage prices, which increased by 2.3%, were the largest contributors to the annual growth rate, with prices in restaurants and hotels also up by 4.8%, SURS said.

In one year, service prices rose by 3.1% on average, while goods prices increased by 1.5%. Within the goods category, non-durable goods saw a 2.1% price increase, semi-durable goods a 1.0% increase, and durable goods a 0.3% price decrease.

In January, consumer prices were on average 0.4% lower compared to December, primarily driven by winter sales of clothing and footwear, with prices for clothing dropping by 10.6%, while footwear was 6.7% cheaper than in the previous month.

Eurozone Shoppers Spent Less as 2024 Ended Amid High Savings Caution
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

Retail spending weakened in the eurozone at the end of last year as consumers continued to prefer to save rather than splurge against a chilly economic backdrop.

Retailers sold 0.2% less in December than in November, a little worse than forecast, figures from EU statistics body Eurostat showed Thursday. November's spending was meanwhile flat, according to new data revising down previous estimates for a slight increase, meaning sales booked no growth during any of the final months of the year.

The lack of growth in retail spending comes in spite of inflation that remained close to the European Central Bank's 2% target throughout last year. Despite that boost to wallets, households in the 20 nations that share the euro showed little sign of wanting to spend rather than save their extra income. The savings rate was over 15% in the third quarter of last year, according to Eurostat's most recent figures, keeping it well above prepandemic trends.

That caution around spending is "due in particular to political and geopolitical uncertainties," economist Laurent Quignon at BNP Paribas wrote in a recent note. Others factors include rising house prices in many of the eurozone's major economies and rising returns on financial investments, which tend to be saved rather than spent, he said.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby
